site.title: NRL TipBot Briefing 2026
site.subhead: Round 12 setup board for tipping, charity betting, and weekly NRL intel
site.intro: A weekly NRL command board for sharper tipping, cleaner bet tracking, and banking any wins for charity.

meta.description: Codex-managed NRL TipBot dashboard for Round 12 preview notes, charity betting discipline, and weekly NRL intel.

ticker: round 12 is a shorter origin-affected slate so every swing game matters more than usual
the mark hughes foundation ledger still sits at 2 and 2 and minus $3.50 because no round 10 or round 11 charity bet was locked
bulldogs get jacob kiraz back while storm lose munster grant and loiero to origin which swings friday night hard
dolphins lose farnworth and still have origin holes but the raiders are missing ethan strange and hudson young too
warriors get roger tuivasa-sheck back and still look the cleaner side against the struggling dragons
cowboys regain taumalolo while south sydney are missing latrell mitchell and campbell graham
no round 12 charity bet is locked yet

featurePanels.title: Lock Pick
items: Bulldogs head to head is the cleanest early read on the reduced slate
Canterbury get Jacob Kiraz back while Melbourne lose Munster, Grant and Loiero to Origin duty
The Dogs do not need to be spectacular to win this version of the matchup
This is the kind of origin-round edge worth respecting before the market fully settles

featurePanels.title: Risk Flags
items: Raiders versus Dolphins is messy because both sides are chopped up by Origin and injuries
Sea Eagles versus Titans has roster noise all over it and feels like a classic trap game
The shortened slate means one upset hurts more than usual
Do not force a charity play just because there are fewer games

featurePanels.title: Weekly Focus
items: Take the clean Origin-depletion reads instead of trying to outsmart the whole round
Watch whether the Bulldogs can actually cash in on Melbourne's missing spine
Trust the Warriors more than the Dragons until St George prove they can punish a reshuffled opponent
See if the Cowboys can handle Souths at home with Taumalolo back in the middle

featurePanels.title: Watchlist
items: Bulldogs look like the most obvious roster-upgrade side on the round
The Dolphins still have enough system strength to be dangerous despite the outs
RTS returning gives the Warriors a higher ceiling immediately
South Sydney's missing stars make the Cowboys game more about discipline than flair

betCard.title: Round 12 Charity Bet
betCard.pick: Bulldogs head to head leads the shortlist
why: Origin outs hit Melbourne harder than Canterbury in this matchup.
Kiraz returning gives the Bulldogs more finishing class on the edge.
If the market does not over-correct, this shapes as the cleanest potential value spot of the week.
risks: Melbourne's system can still cover personnel losses better than most clubs.
The Bulldogs can make life hard for themselves with errors when they should be controlling games.
Without a live price edge, this should stay a shortlist call rather than an automatic bet.

charity.title: Mark Hughes Foundation Ledger
charity.summary: All profits are earmarked for the Mark Hughes Foundation.
notes: Round 5: Newcastle head to head at $2.30 won and collected $11.50.
Round 6: North Queensland Cowboys head to head at $2.00 won and collected $10.00.
Round 7: Wests Tigers head to head at $1.53 lost in a 21-20 defeat to Brisbane.
Round 8: No charity bet placed, bank held at $6.50.
Round 9: Bulldogs head to head at $1.77 lost after a Cowboys comeback win.
Rounds 10 and 11: No charity bet locked, so the ledger stayed unchanged.

watchlist: Whether the Bulldogs price still leaves enough room to justify a $5 charity play.
How much the Dolphins can cover the Farnworth and Hammer absences without losing their edge.
Whether the Titans can steal a game from a badly reshuffled Manly side.
Any late movement on Cowboys versus Rabbitohs once final 19s are confirmed.

sections.title: Situation Report
sections.heading: Round 12 is an Origin week puzzle, not a normal full slate
lines: As of Wednesday, 21 May 2026, official Round 12 team lists and late mail are in and the board has to be read through an Origin-depletion lens.
There are only five games, which means each bad read carries more weight than usual.
The charity ledger is still parked at 2-2 and -$3.50, so patience remains part of the strategy.

sections.title: Bet Analysis
sections.heading: Bulldogs are the first serious charity look of the week
lines: Melbourne lose Cameron Munster, Harry Grant and Trent Loiero to Origin while the Bulldogs get Jacob Kiraz back and keep more of their structure intact.
That does not make Canterbury bulletproof, but it gives them the clearest roster edge of Round 12.
This is still an origin week, so the right move is to wait for a fair price rather than blindly lock a favourite.
If the number is good enough, the Bulldogs are the side most likely to become the week's $5 charity play.

Bulldogs over Storm
Warriors over Dragons
Sea Eagles over Titans
Cowboys over Rabbitohs

sections.title: Round 12 Fixtures

lines: Raiders vs Dolphins
Bulldogs vs Storm
Dragons vs Warriors
Sea Eagles vs Titans
Cowboys vs Rabbitohs

sections.title: Form Radar
sections.heading: Who deserves the upgrade or downgrade this week
lines: Hot: Warriors, Bulldogs in this specific matchup, and a healthier Cowboys middle.
Cold: Dragons, Storm's round-specific depth, and the Rabbitohs' attacking ceiling without Latrell and Campbell Graham.
The biggest trap game on the board is Sea Eagles versus Titans because both line-ups have enough disruption to turn logic upside down.

sections.title: Intel Notes
sections.heading: The data points most worth checking before any charity lock
lines: Herbie Farnworth dropped out late, so the Dolphins still have to win through system rather than star power.
Kiraz back in for Canterbury and the Storm's Origin holes make Friday the cleanest structural edge of the round.
Roger Tuivasa-Sheck's return is the single biggest ceiling boost in Dragons versus Warriors.
South Sydney are missing Latrell Mitchell and Campbell Graham, which matters enough to swing the Cowboys game toward North Queensland.
